{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy-financial/issues/130","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy-financial","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy-financial/issues/130/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy-financial/issues/130/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy-financial/issues/130/events","html_url":"https://github.com/numpy/numpy-financial/issues/130","id":2540715529,"node_id":"I_kwDODJJ2w86XcD4J","number":130,"title":"BUG: npf.pmt() algorithm future value sign is flipped.","user":{"login":"RlndVt","id":1658570,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjE2NTg1NzA=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/1658570?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/RlndVt","html_url":"https://github.com/RlndVt","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/RlndVt/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/RlndVt/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/RlndVt/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/RlndVt/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/RlndVt/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/RlndVt/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/RlndVt/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/RlndVt/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/RlndVt/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":4,"created_at":"2024-09-22T07:04:26Z","updated_at":"2024-11-03T06:50:54Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"### Describe the issue:

When calculating the payment required to end with a remaining open balance, aka with a final_value, the calculated payments are larger than the payments required for a achieving final_value=0.

Workaround is inputting the final_value in a flipped state.

In the current state, if a positive `pv` means a outstanding loan, a negative `fv` means a remaining outstanding debt; whilst a positive `fv` implies having 'overpaid' the loan. This is confusing.

Either the algorithm is wrong, and the sign should be flipped; or the documentation should be updated to clarify that `pv` and `fv`  signs have opposite meaning.

### Reproduce the code example:

```python
import numpy_financial as npf

interest_rate = 0.05 / 12
principal_loan = 200_000
compounding_periods = 360
payment_value_fv0 = npf.pmt(
    interest_rate,
    compounding_periods,
    principal_loan,
    0,
)
payment_value_fv50 = npf.pmt(
    interest_rate,
    compounding_periods,
    principal_loan,
    50_000,
)
payment_value_fvn50 = npf.pmt(
    interest_rate,
    compounding_periods,
    principal_loan,
    -50_000,
)
print("{:.2f}".format(payment_value_fv50))
print("{:.2f}".format(payment_value_fv0))
print("{:.2f}".format(payment_value_fvn50))

assert abs(payment_value_fv50) < abs(payment_value_fv0),\
       "Payment with remaining balance should be lower than fully paying off loan, but found {:.2f} > {:.2f}".format(abs(payment_value_fv50),abs(payment_value_fv0))
```


### Error message:

```
-1133.72
-1073.64
-1013.57
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "[...]/debugPmt.py", line 28, in <module>
    assert abs(payment_value_fv50) < abs(payment_value_fv0),\
           ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
AssertionError: Payment with remaining balance should be lower than fully paying off loan, but found 1133.72 > 1073.64
```
